assertions.expectation: Explains that GKE's maximum upgrade parallelism is approximately 20 nodes simultaneously, regardless of maxSurge setting
assertions.reason: The response mentions maxSurge settings but does not explain GKE's actual 20-node parallelism limit or that maxSurge doesn't override this constraint

assertions.expectation: Calculates that at 20-node parallelism, upgrading 3,000 nodes requires ~150 batches — far more than an 8-hour window allows
assertions.reason: The response acknowledges the timing issue but provides no mathematical calculation showing why 3,000 nodes can't upgrade in 8 hours at GKE's actual parallelism limits

assertions.expectation: Recommends splitting upgrades across multiple maintenance windows (weekends) rather than attempting all at once
assertions.reason: The response explicitly suggests staggering maintenance windows across different weeks and splitting node pools across different maintenance windows

assertions.expectation: Suggests upgrading CPU pools and GPU pools separately, prioritizing CPU pools first as they're less disruptive
assertions.reason: The response recommends separating GPU and CPU clusters and shows staggered scheduling with different pools in different time windows

assertions.expectation: Recommends upgrading GPU pools during scheduled gaps between training/inference workloads
assertions.reason: The response does not mention coordinating GPU upgrades with training/inference workload schedules or gaps between ML workloads

assertions.expectation: Suggests expanding the maintenance window or using multiple consecutive windows for large pool upgrades
assertions.reason: The response suggests extending to a 12-hour window and shows multiple consecutive maintenance windows for different pools

assertions.expectation: Mentions that cluster autoscaler can only scale one node pool at a time — this interacts with upgrade scheduling
assertions.reason: The response does not mention cluster autoscaler's single-pool scaling limitation or how this affects upgrade parallelism

assertions.expectation: Addresses the 15,000 node standard limit and notes GKE 1.31+ supports up to 65,000 for AI workloads
assertions.reason: The response does not mention GKE's node limits, either the 15,000 standard limit or the 65,000 limit for AI workloads in newer versions
